zh.lifeSummary: 當William Nicholas於大約1550年出生在英國時，他的父親William Nichols Royal Blood26歲，母親Hannah Bray21歲。他和Ann Carter至少有3個兒子。他去世於1623年4月20日在英國英格蘭貝德福爾肯普斯頓享年74歲。

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southwestern England and south Wales) and Dutch: from the personal name Nicholas (from Latin Nicolaus, from Greek Nikolaos, from nikān ‘to conquer’ + laos ‘people’). Forms with -ch- are due to hypercorrection (compare Anthony ). The name in various vernacular forms was popular among Christians throughout Europe in the Middle Ages, largely as a result of the fame of a 4th-century Lycian bishop, about whom a large number of legends grew up, and who was venerated in the Orthodox Church as well as the Catholic. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Croatian and Serbian Nikolić (see Nikolic ), Greek Papanikolaou ‘(son of) Nicholas the priest’ and Nikolopoulos . Compare Nickolas .

History: The colonial official and revolutionary patriot Robert Carter Nicholas was from a prominent VA family on both sides. His father was a British navy surgeon who emigrated c. 1700 from Lancashire, England, to Williamsburg, VA.

variants.name: McNichol
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Niocaill, a patronymic from the Gaelic form of Nicholas . This is the usual spelling of the name in Scotland, while it usually ends with double l in Ireland.

variants.name: McNicholas
meanings: Irish: Gaelic patronymic from   Nicholas  . In Ireland this was the surname of a Gaelicized Norman family. Compare   McNichol  .

variants.name: Mastrocola
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Marcello, Vittorio, Carlo, Luigi, Nicola, Rocco, Aldo, Angelo, Armando, Assunta, Camillo, Domenico, Elio, Mario.
Italian (southern): from a title of respect composed of mastro ‘master craftsman’ + Còla, a short form of the personal name Nicola (see Nicholas ).

variants.name: Loos
meanings: German: habitational name from any of several places called Loose or Loosey.
North German: from a short form of Nikolaus, German form of Nicholas .
Flemish and Dutch: nickname from the adjective loos ‘cunning, artful, guileful’.

variants.name: Anthony
meanings: English and West Indian (mainly Antigua and Barbuda, Saint Lucia, and Trinidad and Tobago, also Dutch Caribbean): from the personal name Anthony, Latin Antonius, which, with its variants and cognates, is one of the commonest personal names in Europe. Spellings with -h-, which first appear in English in the 16th century and in French (as Anthoine) at about the same time, are due to the erroneous belief that the name derives from Greek anthos ‘flower’. The popularity of the personal name in Christendom is largely due to the cult of the Egyptian hermit Saint Anthony ( AD 251–356), who in his old age gathered a community of hermits around him, and for that reason is regarded by some as the founder of monasticism. It was further increased by the fame of Saint Anthony of Padua (1195–1231), who long enjoyed a great popular cult and who is believed to help people find lost things. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and derivatives (patronymics) from other languages, for example Greek patronymic Antoniades , Italian Antoni , Polish Antoniewicz , Croatian and Serbian Antonović (see Antonovich ) and Antunović; see also below. The name Anthony is also found among Christians in southern India, but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Antony .

German, Flemish, and French (mainly Alsace): Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from local equivalents of the Latin personal name Antonius, from its genitive form Antoni(i). In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the German, Dutch, French, and Slovak cognates Antoni 1 and Antony 2.
History: John Anthony of Hampstead, Middlesex, England (now part of north London) migrated to Boston, MA, in 1634. By 1640 he had moved to Providence, RI, where his descendants are still established.

variants.name: Loosli
meanings: Swiss German: from a pet form of the personal name Nikolaus (see Nicholas ).

variants.name: Laus
meanings: German and Dutch: from a short form of the personal name Nikolaus, see Nicholas .
Walloon and Flemish: nickname from a Frenchified form of the Dutch adjective loos ‘cunning, artful, guileful’ (compare Loos ), or a cognate of 1 above.
Croatian and Serbian (Lauš): from the personal name Lauš, an old variant of Lajoš, a Slavicized form of Hungarian Lajos, an equivalent of Louis .

variants.name: Lais
meanings: German: perhaps a nickname from Middle High German līse ‘quiet’, or perhaps from a dialect short form of the personal name Nikolaus (see Nicholas ). This surname is found mainly in MN.

variants.name: Lause
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Aloys, Jurgen.
North German: from a short form of the personal name Nikolaus (see Nicholas ).

variants.name: Lauser
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto.
German:
from a short form of the personal name Nikolaus (see Nicholas ).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When William Nicholas was born about 1550, in England, his father, William Nichols Royal Blood, was 26 and his mother, Hannah Bray, was 21. He had at least 3 sons with Ann Carter. He died on 20 April 1623, in Kempston, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Nicholas est né environ 1550, à Angleterre, son père, William Nichols Royal Blood, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Hannah Bray, avait 21 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils avec Ann Carter. Il est décédé le 20 avril 1623, à Kempston, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 74 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Nicholas um 1550 in England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Nichols Royal Blood 26 und seine Mutter Hannah Bray 21. Er hatte mit Ann Carter mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb am 20. April 1623 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Kempston, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Nicholas è nato il circa 1550 nel England, il suo padre, William Nichols Royal Blood, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Hannah Bray, aveva 21 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli con Ann Carter. Morì il 20 aprile 1623, nel Kempston, Bedfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 74 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: William Nicholas(이)가 대략 1550년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Nichols Royal Blood(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Hannah Bray(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Ann Carter(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1623년 4월 20일 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 케프스턴에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Nicholas nasceu aproximadamente 1550, em Inglaterra, seu pai, William Nichols Royal Blood, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Hannah Bray, tinha 21 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos com Ann Carter. Ele faleceu em 20 de abril de 1623, em Kempston, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 74 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Nicholas родился около 1550 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени William Nichols Royal Blood было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Hannah Bray, 21. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына от Ann Carter. Он умер 20 апреля 1623 г. в местности кемпстон, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 74 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Nicholas nació aproximadamente 1550, en Inglaterra, su papá, William Nichols Royal Blood, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Hannah Bray, tenía 21 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos con Ann Carter. Murió el 20 de abril de 1623, en Kempston, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 74 años.
